entities.name: Vrijen Attawar
entities.context: Founder of Careerspan, career coach turned tech entrepreneur

entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's company focused on talent matching and candidate evaluation

entities.name: Free Candidate Side
entities.context: Strategic decision to not charge job seekers; keep candidate experience free

entities.name: Matching as Monetization
entities.context: Core strategy: monetize by being extremely good at matching talent to organizations

entities.name: Ad-hoc Payments
entities.context: Early revenue model with ~$100-$200 payments from ~5 paying customers

entities.name: Careerspan Links
entities.context: Persistent intake funnels for employers/VCs, can be role-specific or general talent calls

entities.name: Candidate Set Shrinking
entities.context: Long-term aim to reduce need for traditional ATS by curating pools to top 2-5%

entities.name: Employer ICP Hypothesis
entities.context: Target customers are employers with meaningful inbound who struggle to filter high applicant volumes

entities.name: ATS Systems
entities.context: Traditional applicant tracking systems that Careerspan aims to reduce the need for

entities.name: Qualitative Fit Scoring
entities.context: Mix of keyword and qualitative fit used to surface top candidates

relationships.context: Intent is to keep the candidate side free, moving away from charging job seekers
Strategy is to monetize by selling high-quality matching capability to organizations
Links act as persistent intake funnels for employers and VCs
Processes inbound interest and surfaces top 2-5% by keyword and qualitative fit
Links funnel candidates through Careerspan's curation to shrink the pool
Long-term aim to reduce reliance on traditional ATS by delivering curated candidate pools
Monetization depends on targeting employers with high inbound who need filtering help
Early ad-hoc revenue is a stepping stone toward the full matching monetization model
